title: Introduction to Test Management Knowledge Check

questions.question: What is test management?
options: Writing software code for automated test scripts
The practice of coordinating, creating, and executing all tests needed to verify product requirements are met
A tool for tracking project deadlines and milestones
The process of deploying products to production environments
questions.rationale: Test management is the practice of coordinating, creating, and executing all tests needed to verify that product requirements have been met. It encompasses planning, designing, executing, and reviewing tests across all levels — not just writing code or tracking schedules.

questions.question: Which role is primarily responsible for writing test plans?
questions.rationale: The Test Lead is responsible for writing test plans, managing the testing team, and coordinating standards and practices. Test Engineers design test cases based on the plan, and Testers execute the tests.

questions.question: What is the correct order of the test management workflow?
options: Design → Plan → Execute → Set Up → Review
Plan → Design → Set Up → Execute → Review
Execute → Design → Plan → Review → Set Up
Set Up → Plan → Design → Review → Execute
questions.rationale: The test management workflow follows five steps: 1) Write a test plan, 2) Design test cases, 3) Set up test runs, 4) Execute tests, 5) Review results. Each step builds on the previous one and involves different roles.

questions.question: In the course project, what is the focus of the vehicle advanced headlight system testing?
options: Designing the electrical circuit for the headlights
Practicing the test management workflow using a realistic product scenario
Programming the headlight control software from scratch
Manufacturing and assembling headlight hardware components
questions.rationale: The course project uses the vehicle advanced headlight system as a realistic scenario to practice test management. You are not expected to know the inner workings of headlight systems — the focus is on the test management workflow: planning, designing test cases, setting up and executing tests, and reporting results.
